meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug Code with Analytics: Advanced Student Guide
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your app fails. Learn how to implement advanced analytics to debug code effectively using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert case study.
content.answer_block: Debugging fails when you lack visibility into runtime state. By integrating event-driven analytics, you transform 'black box' errors into actionable telemetry. Use Antigravity to instrument your core logic and Claude Code to automate the deployment of diagnostic hooks, allowing you to trace execution paths and identify silent failures instantly.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of cryptic stack traces, sweating as your first production-grade feature silently crashes in the wild. The code looks perfect, yet the user experience is broken. Every student faces this 'wall of silence' where traditional debugging tools fail to capture the ephemeral state of a live application.

This isn't a failure of your logic; it's a failure of your visibility. The elite engineers don't guess; they instrument. By architecting an analytics layer, you stop hunting for bugs and start observing them as they manifest, turning every crash into a data point for your next iteration.

steps.title: Define Event Schemas
steps.description: Before instrumenting, map out the critical user flows. Focus on 'entry', 'action', and 'failure' points to ensure your telemetry captures the full lifecycle of a request.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase structure and suggest 5 high-impact event points that capture state transitions before a potential failure occurs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Identify critical state variables in files/auth.ts and generate an analytics event schema'.

steps.title: Instrument Core Logic
steps.description: Inject logging hooks at the boundaries of your functions. This ensures that even if a function fails, the context leading up to the failure is captured.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my current function handlers to include a non-blocking analytics wrapper that captures input arguments and local scope on error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Wrap all exported functions in src/services with the telemetry utility defined in lib/analytics.ts'.

steps.title: Configure Error Boundaries
steps.description: Implement global error boundaries that report to your analytics dashboard, preventing silent crashes and providing a stack trace snapshot.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a unified error handling decorator that sends high-fidelity crash reports including current state and user metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate an ErrorBoundary component that integrates with my analytics provider'.

steps.title: Automate Telemetry Testing
steps.description: Ensure your analytics code is working by simulating failure states in your test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite that mocks a database failure and verifies that the analytics service receives the error event.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create integration tests for error logging in tests/analytics.test.ts'.

steps.title: Synthesize and Optimize
steps.description: Review the captured logs to identify common failure patterns and optimize the code accordingly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the log patterns from the last 24 hours and identify the top 3 causes of runtime crashes in my app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Summarize telemetry logs and suggest code improvements for the most frequent error path'.

pro_tips: Always log the 'before' state of an object, not just the error message, to see what actually changed.
Use sampling for high-frequency events to avoid hitting rate limits and incurring unnecessary cloud costs.
Tag your errors with git commit hashes to instantly correlate a bug with a specific deployment version.
Implement 'breadcrumb' logging so you can reconstruct the user's path leading up to a specific error event.
Never log raw PII (Personally Identifiable Information); always sanitize your event payloads before transmission.
content.next_steps: Once your telemetry is live, set up automated alerting for error spikes and begin refactoring the most frequent failure points identified by your new data layer.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just coding; you are engineering robust systems. This is the difference between a student and a senior developer. Take control of your stack, eliminate the guesswork, and build software that stands up to the real world. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
